description: Knowledge base for platform-wide Stormworks microcontroller behavior that isn't tied to one specific gate/node (tick rate, execution order, composite channel layout, etc.). Uses the same note shape as node-behavior-notes.json.

notes.text: Stormworks' internal processing runs at 60 Hz.
Components other than Video/Audio are each evaluated exactly once per tick. The previous tick's output values are used as this tick's inputs -- a feedback loop's value propagates with a 1-tick delay.
When a monitor is connected, Video components are re-evaluated every rendered frame.
Audio components are also re-evaluated every rendered frame, matching Video.

composite-signal-layout.title: Composite signal channel count and layout

notes.text: DSL-side composite channel numbers are 1-origin, matching the in-game UI and the Lua API.
A Lua script runs once (as initialization) when the vehicle loads, then its onTick is called once per tick thereafter.
onDraw is called once per rendered frame, but only while a monitor is connected. onDraw and onTick are not necessarily synchronized with each other.
A chain of Lua drawing (video) components triggers a "loop" error once the chain length exceeds a threshold.

project-io-limits.title: Microcontroller input/output pin limits, by type

notes.text: Stormworks' own in-game pin-layout panel for the microcontroller (not storm-mcl) only offers a 6x6 grid of slots, so 36 pins is the effective ceiling per signal-type group. `Composite` pins are one group with its own 36-pin ceiling; On/Off (boolean) and Number pins together share a second, separate 36-pin ceiling.

xml-serialization-conventions.title: General omission rules when saving XML

notes.text: Whether a default-valued property is written explicitly or omitted from exported XML varies by property. This variation is normal, not a bug: whichever form appears, it decodes back to the same value.
